itself"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":17,"created_at":"2024-10-05T11:02:34Z","updated_at":"2024-12-01T14:27:40Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"body":"MWE:

```julia
julia> let; 
           function a()
               b()
           end
           function b()
               b = 1
               return b
           end
           @show a()
           @show a()
       end
a() = 1
ERROR: MethodError: objects of type Int64 are not callable
The object of type `Int64` exists, but no method is defined for this combination of argument types when trying to treat it as a callable object.
Maybe you forgot to use an operator such as *, ^, %, / etc. ?
Stacktrace:
 [1] (::var"#a#a##6")()
   @ Main ./REPL[9]:3
 [2] top-level scope
   @ REPL[9]:10
 [3] macro expansion
   @ show.jl:1229 [inlined]
```

As far as I can tell, what's going on is that `a()` and `b()` end up sharing the closed-over `b` in `b()`; either that, or the assignment in `b()` ends up overwriting the binding to `b` in the `let` block, after which the call to `b()` in `a()` fails due to the new object not being a function.

I'd expect this to behave the same as without the `let` block, that is, just print `a() = 1` twice. This was found by a user of Supposition.jl, downstream issue is https://github.com/Seelengrab/Supposition.jl/issues/54. To be clear, the shadowing in `b()` is fine, it's just surprising/unexpected/undesirable to have that shadowing influence what happens in `a()` (which shouldn't care about the shadowing in `b`, and just call the function).

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56740","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56740/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56740/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56740/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56740","id":2714711344,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM6hzzUw","number":56740,"title":"showerror(::MethodError) performance","user":{"login":"green-nsk","id":8025586,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjgwMjU1ODY=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8025586?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/green-nsk","html_url":"https://github.com/green-nsk","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/green-nsk/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/green-nsk/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/green-nsk/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/green-nsk/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/green-nsk/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/green-nsk/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/green-nsk/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/green-nsk/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/green-nsk/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2024-12-03T11:11:04Z","updated_at":"2024-12-03T15:45:59Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"body":"I ran into a problem where `showerror()` will take excessive time when there're a lot of candidates.

```Julia
julia> @time showerror(stdout, MethodError(getproperty, ()))
  ...
  0.007422 seconds (10.27 k allocations: 1012.766 KiB)

julia> using MyProtocol

julia> @time showerror(stdout, MethodError(getproperty, ()))
  ...
 22.118404 seconds (5.21 M allocations: 881.234 MiB, 1.06% gc time, 0.28% compilation time)
```
(there're about 37k overloads on `getproperty` in this example)

The problem is perhaps in [`show_method_candidates()`](https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/v1.10.7/base/errorshow.jl#L391) having some non-linear properties. I wish I could narrow it down further, but parts of it are quite arcane. The blunt solution would be only show method candidates when there'aren't too many candidate overloads.


For context, how come we have so many overloads. We generate protocol some protocol code, which makes message fields appear like properties. Goes something like this:

```Julia
struct MyMessageReader
    data::Ptr{Nothing}
end

@inline Base.getproperty(r::MyMessageReader, p::Symbol) = getproperty(r, Val(p))
@inline Base.getproperty(r::MyMessageReader, ::Val{:int1}) = unsafe_load(convert(Ptr{Int}, r.data))
```

It works fine mostly, but it's quite annoying when you make a typo in a property name, Julia throws `MethodError(getproperty, (MyMessageReader, Val{:my_prop1},))` and subsequent `showerror()` can take very long time. For now, we made `getproperty(r, ::Val)` overloads use module-local name, but it doesn't solve the problem completely because there're still quite a few overloads even within a single protocol (hundreds to thousands).

https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55120

Without something like this, it can be quite tricky to figure out where slow locks are happening.

```julia
julia> Base.slow_lock_warn = 1
1

julia> @sync begin
       l = ReentrantLock()
       @async begin
           lock(l) do
               sleep(2)
           end
       end
       @async begin
           lock(l) do
               sleep(2)
           end
       end
       end
┌ Warning: Taking longer than 1s to get a lock
│   exception =
│    Lock contention warning
│    Stacktrace:
│     [1] (::Base.var"#slowlock#lock##14")(rl::ReentrantLock)
│       @ Base ./REPL[8]:14
│     [2] lock
│       @ ./REPL[8]:2 [inlined]
│     [3] lock(f::var"#47#48", l::ReentrantLock)
│       @ Base ./lock.jl:262
│     [4] (::var"#45#46"{ReentrantLock})()
│       @ Main ./REPL[9]:9
└ @ Base REPL[8]:16
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56744/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56744/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2514960483","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56744#issuecomment-2514960483","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56744","id":2514960483,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6V50Bj","user":{"login":"Seelengrab","id":11753998,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjExNzUzOTk4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/11753998?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab","html_url":"https://github.com/Seelengrab","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-12-03T15:59:20Z","updated_at":"2024-12-03T15:59:20Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"If ```julia
julia> reinterpret(Float64, 0x7ffbb14880000000) |> exp
-9.627794635514914e-309

julia> NaN |> bitstring
"0111111111111000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000"

julia> reinterpret(Float64, 0x7ffbb14880000000) |> bitstring
"0111111111111011101100010100100010000000000000000000000000000000"
```

so a slightly "cleaner" pattern can also serve as a reproducer, as long as the bitpattern is distinct from the usual one:

```julia
julia> @check db=false (f=Data.Floats{Float64}())  -> begin
           uint_float = reinterpret(UInt, f)
           event!(uint_float)
           event!(bitstring(uint_float))
           res = exp(f)
           isnan(res) == isnan(f) || res >= 0
       end
Found counterexample
  Context: ##SuppositionAnon#334

  Arguments:
      f::Float64 = NaN

  Events:
    UNLABELED_EVENT_0
        0x7ff0000000000100
    UNLABELED_EVENT_1
        "0111111111110000000000000000000000000000000000000000000100000000"
```

All of these are proper `NaN` though, and the fix here also makes this randomized test pass :+1: ","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2529279115/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

night I made a type-stable Memoize.jl via a `@generated` function.

Along the way, I tried something that may well be considered undefined behavior, but was surprised to see a `compiler/typeinfer.jl` typeassert error regardless.

```julia-repl
julia> @generated function ohnoes(::F, args...) where {F <: Function}
       Core.Compiler.return_type(F.instance, Tuple{args...}, Base.get_world_counter())
       end
ohnoes (generic function with 1 method)

julia> ohnoes(sum, [1,2,3])
ERROR: TypeError: in typeassert, expected Vector, got a value of type Nothing
Stacktrace:
 [1] return_type(interp::Core.Compiler.NativeInterpreter, t::DataType)
   @ Core.Compiler ./compiler/typeinfer.jl:1187
 [2] return_type(t::DataType, world::UInt64)
   @ Core.Compiler ./compiler/typeinfer.jl:1175
 [3] return_type(f::Any, t::DataType, world::UInt64)
   @ Core.Compiler ./compiler/typeinfer.jl:1165
 [4] #s1#11
   @ ./REPL[40]:2 [inlined]
 [5] var"#s1#11"(F::Any, ::Any, ::Any, args::Any)
   @ Main ./none:0
 [6] (::Core.GeneratedFunctionStub)(::UInt64, ::LineNumberNode, ::Any, ::Vararg{Any})
   @ Core ./boot.jl:707
 [7] top-level scope
   @ REPL[41]:1
```

I'm aware that doing word-age stuff within a `@generated` function is very much not-blessed (not sure if it's explicitly UB from the docs though), but I'm still surprised to see a typeassert error from the compiler.

If there's an invariant the compiler is assuming, I would have thought it would be explicitly checked for, rather than hitting a typeassert at some point.

The line in question is a `_methods_by_ftype` call, which ends up ccall-ing `jl_matching_methods`. The ccall itself is typed to return a `Union{Vector{Any},Nothing}`, but in 10 places (mostly `reflection.jl`, also `bootstrap.jl` and `typeinfer.jl`) a `_methods_by_ftype` call is asserted to be a `Vector`, discarding the `Nothing` case.

Perhaps there's something here that should be more explicitly checked for/errored?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56796/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56796/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2537059956","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56796#issuecomment-2537059956","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56796","id":2537059956,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6XOHZ0","user":{"login":"Seelengrab","id":11753998,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjExNzUzOTk4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/11753998?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab","html_url":"https://github.com/Seelengrab","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-12-11T20:26:52Z","updated_at":"2024-12-11T20:26:52Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Should +@testset "consistency of dict iteration order (issue #56841)" begin
+    dict = Dict(randn() => randn() for _ = 1:100)
+    foreach(zip(dict, keys(dict), values(dict), pairs(dict))) do (d, k, v, p)
+        @test d == p
+        @test first(d) == first(p) == k
+        @test last(d) == last(p) == v
+    end","path":"test/dict.jl","commit_id":"e2bfff5060b82f553a028df009a2ac552bcb01c6","original_commit_id":"e2bfff5060b82f553a028df009a2ac552bcb01c6","user":{"login":"Seelengrab","id":11753998,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjExNzUzOTk4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/11753998?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab","html_url":"https://github.com/Seelengrab","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"In case there's a bug that decouples `keys`/`values`/`iterate`/`pairs`, this will cause 300 individual failures, each showing up in the log. it","user":{"login":"giordano","id":765740,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc2NTc0MA==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/765740?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano","html_url":"https://github.com/giordano","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"Also, add a keyword option to `@testset` to let users override the seed used there, to make testsets more replicable.

This is a draft at the moment because it needs adding tests and docs, but I'm saving my progress so far.  To give you a taster of what this PR enables:
```
julia> using Random, Test

julia> @testset begin
           @test rand() == 0
       end;
test set: Test Failed at REPL[2]:2
  Expression: rand() == 0
   Evaluated: 0.559472630416976 == 0

Stacktrace:
 [1] top-level scope
   @ REPL[2]:2
 [2] macro expansion
   @ ~/repo/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.12/Test/src/Test.jl:1713 [inlined]
 [3] macro expansion
   @ REPL[2]:2 [inlined]
 [4] macro expansion
   @ ~/repo/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.12/Test/src/Test.jl:679 [inlined]
Test Summary: | Fail  Total  Time
test set      |    1      1  0.9s
ERROR: Some tests did not pass: 0 passed, 1 failed, 0 errored, 0 broken.
Random seed for this testset: Xoshiro(0x2e026445595ed28e, 0x07bb81ac4c54926d, 0x83d7d70843e8bad6, 0xdbef927d150af80b, 0xdbf91ddf2534f850)

julia> @testset seed=Xoshiro(0x2e026445595ed28e, 0x07bb81ac4c54926d, 0x83d7d70843e8bad6, 0xdbef927d150af80b, 0xdbf91ddf2534f850) begin
           @test rand() == 0.559472630416976
       end;
Test Summary: | Pass  Total  Time
test set      |    1      1  0.0s
```
This also works with nested testsets, and testsets on for loops:
```
julia> @testset seed=Xoshiro(0xc380f460355639ee, 0xb39bc754b7d63bbf, 0x1551dbcfb5ed5668, 0x71ab5a18fec21a25, 0x649d0c1be1ca5436) "Outer" begin
           @test rand() == 0.0004120194925605336
           @testset seed=Xoshiro(0xee97f5b53f7cdc49, 0x480ac387b0527d3d, 0x614b416502a9e0f5, 0x5250cb36e4a4ceb1, 0xed6615c59e475fa0) "Inner: $(i)" for i in 1:10
               @test rand() == 0.39321938407066637
           end
       end;
Test Summary: | Pass  Total  Time
Outer         |   11     11  0.0s
```

Being able to see what was the seed inside a testset and being able to set it afterwards should make replicating test failures which only depend on the state of the RNG much easier to add a keyword option to `@testset` to let users override the seed used there, to make testsets more replicable.

This is a draft at the moment because it needs adding tests and docs, but I'm saving my progress so far.  To give you a taster of what this PR enables:
```
julia> using Random, Test

julia> @testset begin
           @test rand() == 0
       end;
test set: Test Failed at REPL[2]:2
  Expression: rand() == 0
   Evaluated: 0.559472630416976 == 0

Stacktrace:
 [1] top-level scope
   @ REPL[2]:2
 [2] macro expansion
   @ ~/repo/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.12/Test/src/Test.jl:1713 [inlined]
 [3] macro expansion
   @ REPL[2]:2 [inlined]
 [4] macro expansion
   @ ~/repo/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.12/Test/src/Test.jl:679 [inlined]
Test Summary: | Fail  Total  Time
test set      |    1      1  0.9s
ERROR: Some tests did not pass: 0 passed, 1 failed, 0 errored, 0 broken.
Random seed for this testset: Xoshiro(0x2e026445595ed28e, 0x07bb81ac4c54926d, 0x83d7d70843e8bad6, 0xdbef927d150af80b, 0xdbf91ddf2534f850)

julia> @testset seed=Xoshiro(0x2e026445595ed28e, 0x07bb81ac4c54926d, 0x83d7d70843e8bad6, 0xdbef927d150af80b, 0xdbf91ddf2534f850) begin
           @test rand() == 0.559472630416976
       end;
Test Summary: | Pass  Total  Time
test set      |    1      1  0.0s
```
This also works with nested testsets, and testsets on for loops:
```
julia> @testset seed=Xoshiro(0xc380f460355639ee, 0xb39bc754b7d63bbf, 0x1551dbcfb5ed5668, 0x71ab5a18fec21a25, 0x649d0c1be1ca5436) "Outer" begin
           @test rand() == 0.0004120194925605336
           @testset seed=Xoshiro(0xee97f5b53f7cdc49, 0x480ac387b0527d3d, 0x614b416502a9e0f5, 0x5250cb36e4a4ceb1, 0xed6615c59e475fa0) "Inner: $(i)" for i in 1:10
               @test rand() == 0.39321938407066637
           end
       end;
Test Summary: | Pass  Total  Time
Outer         |   11     11  0.0s
```

Being able to see what was the seed inside a testset and being able to set it afterwards should make replicating test failures which only depend on the state of the RNG much easier to :"[Test] add a keyword option to `@testset` to let users override the seed used there, to make testsets more replicable.

This is a draft at the moment because it needs adding tests and docs, but I'm saving my progress so far.  To give you a taster of what this PR enables:
```
julia> using Random, Test

julia> @testset begin
           @test rand() == 0
       end;
test set: Test Failed at REPL[2]:2
  Expression: rand() == 0
   Evaluated: 0.559472630416976 == 0

Stacktrace:
 [1] top-level scope
   @ REPL[2]:2
 [2] macro expansion
   @ ~/repo/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.12/Test/src/Test.jl:1713 [inlined]
 [3] macro expansion
   @ REPL[2]:2 [inlined]
 [4] macro expansion
   @ ~/repo/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.12/Test/src/Test.jl:679 [inlined]
Test Summary: | Fail  Total  Time
test set      |    1      1  0.9s
ERROR: Some tests did not pass: 0 passed, 1 failed, 0 errored, 0 broken.
Random seed for this testset: Xoshiro(0x2e026445595ed28e, 0x07bb81ac4c54926d, 0x83d7d70843e8bad6, 0xdbef927d150af80b, 0xdbf91ddf2534f850)

julia> @testset seed=Xoshiro(0x2e026445595ed28e, 0x07bb81ac4c54926d, 0x83d7d70843e8bad6, 0xdbef927d150af80b, 0xdbf91ddf2534f850) begin
           @test rand() == 0.559472630416976
       end;
Test Summary: | Pass  Total  Time
test set      |    1      1  0.0s
```
This also works with nested testsets, and testsets on for loops:
```
julia> @testset seed=Xoshiro(0xc380f460355639ee, 0xb39bc754b7d63bbf, 0x1551dbcfb5ed5668, 0x71ab5a18fec21a25, 0x649d0c1be1ca5436) "Outer" begin
           @test rand() == 0.0004120194925605336
           @testset seed=Xoshiro(0xee97f5b53f7cdc49, 0x480ac387b0527d3d, 0x614b416502a9e0f5, 0x5250cb36e4a4ceb1, 0xed6615c59e475fa0) "Inner: $(i)" for i in 1:10
               @test rand() == 0.39321938407066637
           end
       end;
Test Summary: | Pass  Total  Time
Outer         |   11     11  0.0s
```

Being able to see what was the seed inside a testset and being able to set it afterwards should make replicating test failures which only depend on the state of the RNG much easier to add a keyword option to `@testset` to let users override the seed used there, to make testsets more replicable.

This is a draft at the moment because it needs adding tests and docs, but I'm saving my progress so far.  To give you a taster of what this PR enables:
```
julia> using Random, Test

julia> @testset begin
           @test rand() == 0
       end;
test set: Test Failed at REPL[2]:2
  Expression: rand() == 0
   Evaluated: 0.559472630416976 == 0

Stacktrace:
 [1] top-level scope
   @ REPL[2]:2
 [2] macro expansion
   @ ~/repo/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.12/Test/src/Test.jl:1713 [inlined]
 [3] macro expansion
   @ REPL[2]:2 [inlined]
 [4] macro expansion
   @ ~/repo/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.12/Test/src/Test.jl:679 [inlined]
Test Summary: | Fail  Total  Time
test set      |    1      1  0.9s
ERROR: Some tests did not pass: 0 passed, 1 failed, 0 errored, 0 broken.
Random seed for this testset: Xoshiro(0x2e026445595ed28e, 0x07bb81ac4c54926d, 0x83d7d70843e8bad6, 0xdbef927d150af80b, 0xdbf91ddf2534f850)

julia> @testset seed=Xoshiro(0x2e026445595ed28e, 0x07bb81ac4c54926d, 0x83d7d70843e8bad6, 0xdbef927d150af80b, 0xdbf91ddf2534f850) begin
           @test rand() == 0.559472630416976
       end;
Test Summary: | Pass  Total  Time
test set      |    1      1  0.0s
```
This also works with nested testsets, and testsets on for loops:
```
julia> @testset seed=Xoshiro(0xc380f460355639ee, 0xb39bc754b7d63bbf, 0x1551dbcfb5ed5668, 0x71ab5a18fec21a25, 0x649d0c1be1ca5436) "Outer" begin
           @test rand() == 0.0004120194925605336
           @testset seed=Xoshiro(0xee97f5b53f7cdc49, 0x480ac387b0527d3d, 0x614b416502a9e0f5, 0x5250cb36e4a4ceb1, 0xed6615c59e475fa0) "Inner: $(i)" for i in 1:10
               @test rand() == 0.39321938407066637
           end
       end;
Test Summary: | Pass  Total  Time
Outer         |   11     11  0.0s
```

Being able to see what was the seed inside a testset and being able to set it afterwards should make replicating test failures which only depend on the state of the RNG much easier to :"[Test] it","user":{"login":"giordano","id":765740,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc2NTc0MA==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/765740?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano","html_url":"https://github.com/giordano","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"Also, add a keyword option to `@testset` to let users override the seed used there, to make testsets more replicable.

This is a draft at the moment because it needs adding tests and docs, but I'm saving my progress so far.  To give you a taster of what this PR enables:
```
julia> using Random, Test

julia> @testset begin
           @test rand() == 0
       end;
test set: Test Failed at REPL[2]:2
  Expression: rand() == 0
   Evaluated: 0.559472630416976 == 0

Stacktrace:
 [1] top-level scope
   @ REPL[2]:2
 [2] macro expansion
   @ ~/repo/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.12/Test/src/Test.jl:1713 [inlined]
 [3] macro expansion
   @ REPL[2]:2 [inlined]
 [4] macro expansion
   @ ~/repo/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.12/Test/src/Test.jl:679 [inlined]
Test Summary: | Fail  Total  Time
test set      |    1      1  0.9s
ERROR: Some tests did not pass: 0 passed, 1 failed, 0 errored, 0 broken.
Random seed for this testset: Xoshiro(0x2e026445595ed28e, 0x07bb81ac4c54926d, 0x83d7d70843e8bad6, 0xdbef927d150af80b, 0xdbf91ddf2534f850)

julia> @testset seed=Xoshiro(0x2e026445595ed28e, 0x07bb81ac4c54926d, 0x83d7d70843e8bad6, 0xdbef927d150af80b, 0xdbf91ddf2534f850) begin
           @test rand() == 0.559472630416976
       end;
Test Summary: | Pass  Total  Time
test set      |    1      1  0.0s
```
This also works with nested testsets, and testsets on for loops:
```
julia> @testset seed=Xoshiro(0xc380f460355639ee, 0xb39bc754b7d63bbf, 0x1551dbcfb5ed5668, 0x71ab5a18fec21a25, 0x649d0c1be1ca5436) "Outer" begin
           @test rand() == 0.0004120194925605336
           @testset seed=Xoshiro(0xee97f5b53f7cdc49, 0x480ac387b0527d3d, 0x614b416502a9e0f5, 0x5250cb36e4a4ceb1, 0xed6615c59e475fa0) "Inner: $(i)" for i in 1:10
               @test rand() == 0.39321938407066637
           end
       end;
Test Summary: | Pass  Total  Time
Outer         |   11     11  0.0s
```

Being able to see what was the seed inside a testset and being able to set it afterwards should make replicating test failures which only depend on the state of the RNG much easier to :"[Test] it","user":{"login":"giordano","id":765740,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc2NTc0MA==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/765740?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano","html_url":"https://github.com/giordano","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"Also, add a keyword option to `@testset` to let users override the seed used there, to make testsets more replicable.

This is a draft at the moment because it needs adding tests and docs, but I'm saving my progress so far.  To give you a taster of what this PR enables:
```
julia> using Random, Test

julia> @testset begin
           @test rand() == 0
       end;
test set: Test Failed at REPL[2]:2
  Expression: rand() == 0
   Evaluated: 0.559472630416976 == 0

Stacktrace:
 [1] top-level scope
   @ REPL[2]:2
 [2] macro expansion
   @ ~/repo/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.12/Test/src/Test.jl:1713 [inlined]
 [3] macro expansion
   @ REPL[2]:2 [inlined]
 [4] macro expansion
   @ ~/repo/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.12/Test/src/Test.jl:679 [inlined]
Test Summary: | Fail  Total  Time
test set      |    1      1  0.9s
ERROR: Some tests did not pass: 0 passed, 1 failed, 0 errored, 0 broken.
Random seed for this testset: Xoshiro(0x2e026445595ed28e, 0x07bb81ac4c54926d, 0x83d7d70843e8bad6, 0xdbef927d150af80b, 0xdbf91ddf2534f850)

julia> @testset seed=Xoshiro(0x2e026445595ed28e, 0x07bb81ac4c54926d, 0x83d7d70843e8bad6, 0xdbef927d150af80b, 0xdbf91ddf2534f850) begin
           @test rand() == 0.559472630416976
       end;
Test Summary: | Pass  Total  Time
test set      |    1      1  0.0s
```
This also works with nested testsets, and testsets on for loops:
```
julia> @testset seed=Xoshiro(0xc380f460355639ee, 0xb39bc754b7d63bbf, 0x1551dbcfb5ed5668, 0x71ab5a18fec21a25, 0x649d0c1be1ca5436) "Outer" begin
           @test rand() == 0.0004120194925605336
           @testset seed=Xoshiro(0xee97f5b53f7cdc49, 0x480ac387b0527d3d, 0x614b416502a9e0f5, 0x5250cb36e4a4ceb1, 0xed6615c59e475fa0) "Inner: $(i)" for i in 1:10
               @test rand() == 0.39321938407066637
           end
       end;
Test Summary: | Pass  Total  Time
Outer         |   11     11  0.0s
```

Being able to see what was the seed inside a testset and being able to set it afterwards should make replicating test failures which only depend on the state of the RNG much easier to I've hit that exact "bug" in Supposition.jl too, which is exactly why I now seed the default RNG from the one I provide :) 

One case that this still doesn't (and can't) cover is when a library doesn't use the default RNG at all, but rather some other source of randomness (e.g. as happened [here](https://github.com/Seelengrab/Supposition.jl/issues/56#issuecomment-2530505150)). [Arpack](https://arpack.julialinearalgebra.org/stable/) for example uses  an RNG internally, but that one doesn't seem to be seedable from Julia, so even with the feature from this PR, failures involving this library are going to be non-deterministic.","created_at":"2024-12-30T21:58:23Z","updated_at":"2024-12-30T21:58:23Z","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56260#discussion_r1899815703","pull_request_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/56260","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","_links":{"self":{"href":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/comments/1899815703"},"html":{"href":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56260#discussion_r1899815703"},"pull_request":{"href":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/56260"}},"reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/comments/1899815703/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"start_line":null,"original_start_line":null,"start_side":null,"line":1761,"original_line":1758,"side":"RIGHT","in_reply_to_id":1899715768,"original_position":102,"position":120,"subject_type":"line"},"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/56260","id":2134814709,"node_id":"PR_kwDOABkWpM5_Pq_1","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56260","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56260.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56260.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56260","number":56260,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"[Test] it","user":{"login":"giordano","id":765740,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc2NTc0MA==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/765740?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano","html_url":"https://github.com/giordano","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/giordano/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"Also, add a keyword option to `@testset` to let users override the seed used there, to make testsets more replicable.

This is a draft at the moment because it needs adding tests and docs, but I'm saving my progress so far.  To give you a taster of what this PR enables:
```
julia> using Random, Test

julia> @testset begin
           @test rand() == 0
       end;
test set: Test Failed at REPL[2]:2
  Expression: rand() == 0
   Evaluated: 0.559472630416976 == 0

Stacktrace:
 [1] top-level scope
   @ REPL[2]:2
 [2] macro expansion
   @ ~/repo/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.12/Test/src/Test.jl:1713 [inlined]
 [3] macro expansion
   @ REPL[2]:2 [inlined]
 [4] macro expansion
   @ ~/repo/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.12/Test/src/Test.jl:679 [inlined]
Test Summary: | Fail  Total  Time
test set      |    1      1  0.9s
ERROR: Some tests did not pass: 0 passed, 1 failed, 0 errored, 0 broken.
Random seed for this testset: Xoshiro(0x2e026445595ed28e, 0x07bb81ac4c54926d, 0x83d7d70843e8bad6, 0xdbef927d150af80b, 0xdbf91ddf2534f850)

julia> @testset seed=Xoshiro(0x2e026445595ed28e, 0x07bb81ac4c54926d, 0x83d7d70843e8bad6, 0xdbef927d150af80b, 0xdbf91ddf2534f850) begin
           @test rand() == 0.559472630416976
       end;
Test Summary: | Pass  Total  Time
test set      |    1      1  0.0s
```
This also works with nested testsets, and testsets on for loops:
```
julia> @testset seed=Xoshiro(0xc380f460355639ee, 0xb39bc754b7d63bbf, 0x1551dbcfb5ed5668, 0x71ab5a18fec21a25, 0x649d0c1be1ca5436) "Outer" begin
           @test rand() == 0.0004120194925605336
           @testset seed=Xoshiro(0xee97f5b53f7cdc49, 0x480ac387b0527d3d, 0x614b416502a9e0f5, 0x5250cb36e4a4ceb1, 0xed6615c59e475fa0) "Inner: $(i)" for i in 1:10
               @test rand() == 0.39321938407066637
           end
       end;
Test Summary: | Pass  Total  Time
Outer         |   11     11  0.0s
```

Being able to see what was the seed inside a testset and being able to set it afterwards should make replicating test failures which only depend on the state of the RNG much easier to {"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56680","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56680/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56680/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56680/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56680","id":2693711766,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM6gjseW","number":56680,"title":"`ispunct('+')` return false","user":{"login":"inkydragon","id":5158738,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjUxNTg3Mzg=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5158738?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/inkydragon","html_url":"https://github.com/inkydragon","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/inkydragon/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/inkydragon/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/inkydragon/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/inkydragon/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/inkydragon/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/inkydragon/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/inkydragon/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/inkydragon/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/inkydragon/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-11-26T08:42:02Z","updated_at":"2024-11-26T16:10:19Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Not sure if this is a bug.
This seems intentional, but the `ispunct` in julia is inconsistent with the behavior of the C function of the same name, which is confusing.

Perhaps a warning could be added to clarify the inconsistency with C.

https://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/string/byte/ispunct

```jl
julia> c = '+'
'+': ASCII/Unicode U+002B (category Sm: Symbol, math)

julia> ispunct(c)
false

julia> ( @ccall ispunct(c::Cchar)::Cint ) != 0
true

julia> c = '-'
'-': ASCII/Unicode U+002D (category Pd: Punctuation, dash)

julia> (ispunct(c), ( @ccall ispunct(c::Cchar)::Cint )!= 0)
(true, true)
```

```jl
  ispunct(c::AbstractChar) -> Bool

  Tests whether a character belongs to the Unicode general category Punctuation, i.e. a character whose category code
  begins with 'P'.
```

And more chars:

```jl
julia> for c in raw"""!"#$%&'()*+,-./:;<=>?@[\]^_`{|}~"""
           ispunct(c) || println(c)
       end
$
+
<
=
>
^
`
|
~
```

This seems intentional, but the `ispunct` in julia is inconsistent with the behavior of the C function of the same name, which is confusing.

Perhaps a warning could be added to clarify the inconsistency with C.

https://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/string/byte/ispunct

```jl
julia> c = '+'
'+': ASCII/Unicode U+002B (category Sm: Symbol, math)

julia> ispunct(c)
false

julia> ( @ccall ispunct(c::Cchar)::Cint ) != 0
true

julia> c = '-'
'-': ASCII/Unicode U+002D (category Pd: Punctuation, dash)

julia> (ispunct(c), ( @ccall ispunct(c::Cchar)::Cint )!= 0)
(true, true)
```

```jl
  ispunct(c::AbstractChar) -> Bool

  Tests whether a character belongs to the Unicode general category Punctuation, i.e. a character whose category code
  begins with 'P'.
```

And more chars:

```jl
julia> for c in raw"""!"#$%&'()*+,-./:;<=>?@[\]^_`{|}~"""
           ispunct(c) || println(c)
       end
$
+
<
=
>
^
`
|
~
```

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56002","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56002/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56002/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56002/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56002","id":2567866417,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM6ZDogx","number":56002,"title":"Shadowing in closures in `let` blocks influences bindings in neighboring functions","user":{"login":"Seelengrab","id":11753998,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjExNzUzOTk4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/11753998?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab","html_url":"https://github.com/Seelengrab","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2024-10-05T11:02:34Z","updated_at":"2024-10-06T06:25:20Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"MWE:

```julia
julia> let; 
           function a()
               b()
           end
           function b()
               b = 1
               return b
           end
           @show a()
           @show a()
       end
a() = 1
ERROR: MethodError: objects of type Int64 are not callable
The object of type `Int64` exists, but no method is defined for this combination of argument types when trying to treat it as a callable object.
Maybe you forgot to use an operator such as *, ^, %, / etc. ?
Stacktrace:
 [1] (::var"#a#a##6")()
   @ Main ./REPL[9]:3
 [2] top-level scope
   @ REPL[9]:10
 [3] macro expansion
   @ show.jl:1229 [inlined]
```

As far as I can tell, what's going on is that `a()` and `b()` end up sharing the closed-over `b` in `b()`; either that, or the assignment in `b()` ends up overwriting the binding to `b` in the `let` block, after which the call to `b()` in `a()` fails due to the new object not being a function.

I'd expect this to behave the same as without the `let` block, that is, just print `a() = 1` twice. This was found by a user of Supposition.jl, downstream issue is https://github.com/Seelengrab/Supposition.jl/issues/54. To be clear, the shadowing in `b()` is fine, it's just surprising/unexpected/undesirable to have that shadowing influence what happens in `a()` (which shouldn't care about the shadowing in `b`, and just call the function).

I'm not sure I understand what you're trying to say, this is not the usual terminology. All non-`const` bindings in julia are reassignable, the question is just which scope you end up assigning into. Do you mean to say that in a local scope, bindings inherited from a parent scope can be assigned to? If so, ok, but that's exactly the point I'm trying to make - I'm arguing that this _shouldn't_ happen for function definitions (or the internally generated closure name) and that those names should completely shadow the definition of the parent scope, rather than reassign it. Surely reassigning a function from within itself and having *that* show up in yet another function is an unintended consequence of the current scoping rules? Note that due to the way closures are lowered, this even ends up with `Core.Box`es everywhere! None of that happens in global scope, where it's just a `GlobalRef` in `a` that's completely shadowed (and thus unaffected) within `b()`.

I also want to point out that as a package author, there is pretty much nothing I can do to guard against this. The original MWE (involving `@testset`) did not make it clear that the expression suddenly moved into a local scope. How do you propose I fix this on my end?

----

Nit: Regardless of this being a bug or not, I picked the `compiler: lowering` label to communicate that this related to variable scoping, which is decided during lowering. I don't understand why you removed the label? itself"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":8,"created_at":"2024-10-05T11:02:34Z","updated_at":"2024-10-07T05:39:02Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"MWE:

```julia
julia> let; 
           function a()
               b()
           end
           function b()
               b = 1
               return b
           end
           @show a()
           @show a()
       end
a() = 1
ERROR: MethodError: objects of type Int64 are not callable
The object of type `Int64` exists, but no method is defined for this combination of argument types when trying to treat it as a callable object.
Maybe you forgot to use an operator such as *, ^, %, / etc. ?
Stacktrace:
 [1] (::var"#a#a##6")()
   @ Main ./REPL[9]:3
 [2] top-level scope
   @ REPL[9]:10
 [3] macro expansion
   @ show.jl:1229 [inlined]
```

As far as I can tell, what's going on is that `a()` and `b()` end up sharing the closed-over `b` in `b()`; either that, or the assignment in `b()` ends up overwriting the binding to `b` in the `let` block, after which the call to `b()` in `a()` fails due to the new object not being a function.

I'd expect this to behave the same as without the `let` block, that is, just print `a() = 1` twice. This was found by a user of Supposition.jl, downstream issue is https://github.com/Seelengrab/Supposition.jl/issues/54. To be clear, the shadowing in `b()` is fine, it's just surprising/unexpected/undesirable to have that shadowing influence what happens in `a()` (which shouldn't care about the shadowing in `b`, and just call the function).

From the POV of dev-UX, this is an antipattern, since it subtly changes the meaning of code inside. IMO it'd be better to have individual testsets more isolated; introducing a new global scope would be fine. That's also what TestItems.jl does (I don't use it, but lots of people seem to enjoy the features it has).

> I think this is more or less the same issue as #14948, although a slightly different manifestation ofc.

Thank you, that indeed seems related! In particular the [discourse thread](https://discourse.julialang.org/t/scope-in-functions/22096/12?u=sukera) at the end is interesting, because this issue is an example where moving code around definitely changes what the code means.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2395951436/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

 [2] top-level scope
   @ none:1
```

code"},{"id":371587497,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwzNzE1ODc0OTc=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/design","name":"design","color":"e99695","default":false,"description":"Design of itself"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":13,"created_at":"2024-10-05T11:02:34Z","updated_at":"2024-10-10T21:13:10Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"MWE:

```julia
julia> let; 
           function a()
               b()
           end
           function b()
               b = 1
               return b
           end
           @show a()
           @show a()
       end
a() = 1
ERROR: MethodError: objects of type Int64 are not callable
The object of type `Int64` exists, but no method is defined for this combination of argument types when trying to treat it as a callable object.
Maybe you forgot to use an operator such as *, ^, %, / etc. ?
Stacktrace:
 [1] (::var"#a#a##6")()
   @ Main ./REPL[9]:3
 [2] top-level scope
   @ REPL[9]:10
 [3] macro expansion
   @ show.jl:1229 [inlined]
```

As far as I can tell, what's going on is that `a()` and `b()` end up sharing the closed-over `b` in `b()`; either that, or the assignment in `b()` ends up overwriting the binding to `b` in the `let` block, after which the call to `b()` in `a()` fails due to the new object not being a function.

I'd expect this to behave the same as without the `let` block, that is, just print `a() = 1` twice. This was found by a user of Supposition.jl, downstream issue is https://github.com/Seelengrab/Supposition.jl/issues/54. To be clear, the shadowing in `b()` is fine, it's just surprising/unexpected/undesirable to have that shadowing influence what happens in `a()` (which shouldn't care about the shadowing in `b`, and just call the function).

also https://github.com/JuliaDocs/Documenter.jl/issues/2160

```julia
julia> const m = Module()
Main.anonymous

julia> @eval m begin
           abstract type Foo end
           struct Bar <: Foo end
       end

julia> supertype(m.Bar) === m.Foo
true

julia> subtypes(m.Foo)
Any[]
```

`subtypes` should  obviously contain `m.Bar` as well.      unsafe_crc32c(s, sizeof(s) % Csize_t, crc)

+function _crc32c(a::AbstractVector{UInt8}, crc::UInt32=0x00000000)
+    # use block size 24576=8192*3, since that is the threshold for
+    # 3-way parallel SIMD code in the underlying jl_crc32c C function.
+    last = lastindex(a)
+    nb = length(a)
+    buf = Vector{UInt8}(undef, min(nb, collection"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-09-01T11:03:22Z","updated_at":"2024-09-01T16:05:25Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"This might be too ambitious, but broadcasting a function that may conditionally preserve zeros fails currently:
```julia
julia> (x -> rand() < 0.5 ? 0 : 1).(Diagonal([1,2]))
ERROR: ArgumentError: cannot set off-diagonal entry (2, 1) to a nonzero value (1)
Stacktrace:
  [1] setindex!
    @ ~/.julia/juliaup/julia-nightly/share/julia/stdlib/v1.12/LinearAlgebra/src/diagonal.jl:206 [inlined]
  [2] _setindex!
    @ ./abstractarray.jl:1472 [inlined]
  [3] setindex!
    @ ./abstractarray.jl:1442 [inlined]
  [4] macro expansion
    @ ./broadcast.jl:974 [inlined]
  [5] macro expansion
    @ ./simdloop.jl:77 [inlined]
  [6] copyto!
    @ ./broadcast.jl:973 [inlined]
  [7] copyto!(dest::Diagonal{…}, bc::Base.Broadcast.Broadcasted{…})
    @ LinearAlgebra ~/.julia/juliaup/julia-nightly/share/julia/stdlib/v1.12/LinearAlgebra/src/structuredbroadcast.jl:206
  [8] copy
    @ ./broadcast.jl:898 [inlined]
  [9] materialize(bc::Base.Broadcast.Broadcasted{LinearAlgebra.StructuredMatrixStyle{…}, Nothing, var"#35#36", Tuple{…}})
    @ Base.Broadcast ./broadcast.jl:873
 [10] top-level scope
    @ REPL[2]:1
Some type information was truncated. Use `show(err)` to see complete types.

julia> (x -> rand() < 0.5 ? 0 : 1).(Diagonal([1,2]))
2×2 Matrix{Int64}:
 0  0
 1  0

julia> versioninfo()
Julia Version 1.12.0-DEV.1127
Commit ca72e28f26b (2024-08-31 11:47 UTC)
Build Info:
  Official https://julialang.org release
Platform Info:
  OS: Linux (x86_64-linux-gnu)
  CPU: 8 × Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-10310U CPU @ 1.70GHz
  WORD_SIZE: 64
  LLVM: libLLVM-18.1.7 (ORCJIT, skylake)
Threads: 1 default, 0 interactive, 1 GC (on 8 virtual cores)
Environment:
  JULIA_EDITOR = subl
``` 
This is because the function is evaluated once to check if it does preserve a zero, and the destination type is chosen based on this. In the first case, the destination is chosen to be a `Diagonal`, which fails, while in the second case, it is chosen to be a `Matrix` and the `copyto!` succeeds. This approach of choosing the destination by evaluating the function only works if the function always produces the same output for a zero input. I wonder if there might be some introspection tools that might help us assess this? Or at least, this should consistently error, so that a working code doesn't break on being run a second time.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55662/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55662/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2323408856","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55662#issuecomment-2323408856","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55662","id":2323408856,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6KfGfY","user":{"login":"Seelengrab","id":11753998,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjExNzUzOTk4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/11753998?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab","html_url":"https://github.com/Seelengrab","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-09-01T16:05:24Z","updated_at":"2024-09-01T16:05:24Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"> This is because the function is evaluated once to check if it does preserve a zero, and the destination type is chosen based on this.

2. How you installed Julia:

3. MWE
c_code.c:
```c
#include <stdio.h>
#include <pthread.h>
#include <semaphore.h>

sem_t sem;
void c_sem_init() {
    sem_init(&sem, 0, 0);  // Initialize semaphore with a value of 0
}
void c_function_with_wait() {
    int res = sem_wait(&sem);
    if (res != 0) {
        perror("sem_wait failed");
    }
}
void c_function_with_post() {
    int res = sem_post(&sem);
    if (res != 0) {
        perror("sem_post failed");
    }
}
``` 

julia_c_code.jl:
```julia
# Define the C function signature
using Base.Threads;

function wait_on_semaphore()
    ccall((:c_function_with_wait, "libinfinite.so"), Cvoid, ())
end

function post_to_semaphore()
    ccall((:c_function_with_post, "libinfinite.so"), Cvoid, ())
end

gc_state = @ccall(jl_gc_safe_enter()::Int8)
ccall((:c_sem_init, "libinfinite.so"), Cvoid, ())
# Create tasks that call the respective functions
task1 = @spawn wait_on_semaphore()
println(Core.stdout, "Waiting on semaphore")
sleep(1)  # Ensure task1 runs and waits on the semaphore

println(Core.stdout, "Now posting on semaphore")
task2 = @spawn post_to_semaphore()
println(Core.stdout, "Posted on semaphore")

# Wait for both tasks to complete
wait(task1)
wait(task2)
@ccall(jl_gc_safe_leave(gc_state::Int8)::Cvoid)
```

Makefile:

    gcc -g -O0 -fPIC -shared  -o libinfinite.so c_code.c -I$(JULIA)/include/julia -L$(JULIA)/lib -ljulia -lpthread
run:

```

","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55635/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55635/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2323421932","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55635#issuecomment-2323421932","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55635","id":2323421932,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6KfJrs","user":{"login":"Seelengrab","id":11753998,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjExNzUzOTk4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/11753998?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab","html_url":"https://github.com/Seelengrab","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-09-01T16:47:33Z","updated_at":"2024-09-01T16:47:33Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"> In this scenario, we have asked to create two threads one thread is waiting on the semaphore and another thread is posting semaphore.

I think there is a misunderstanding here (I blame the name of the standard library) - `@spawn` _does not_ create new OS threads, it spawns a julia `Task` that is then executed on the available OS threads that julia was spawned with. These are sometimes called green threads, or an n:m threading model (n tasks scheduled on m OS threads).

>Even if we remove sleep statement, it still hangs. The execution behavior of Julia scheduler for threads is creating the deadlock situation

Yes, as mentioned above, the `sem_wait` in your C code blocks the sole available OS thread (the one the scheduler itself also runs on!) and doesn't yield to the julia scheduler. Because of this, your julia code doesn't get scheduled again and nothing ends up signaling the semaphore, resulting in the deadlock.

In other words, there's nothing the scheduler can do about this, it doesn't even get to run again.

julia> a ≥ b # true
julia> a > b # true
julia> b > a # false
julia> b ≥ a # false
julia> b ≥ Dates.Minute(0) # false

This seems to generate allocations that scale with the size of the array:
```julia
function iterate_slices!(array, d)
    for slice in eachslice(array, dims=d)
        nothing
    end
end

tempArray = fill(0, 90, 6);
@time iterate_slices!(tempArray, 1)
# 0.000017 seconds (184 allocations: 2.891 KiB)
```

However, when I fix the `dims` keyword, allocations disappear:
```julia
function iterate_slices2!(array, d)
    for slice in eachslice(array, dims=1)
        nothing
    end
end

@time iterate_slices2!(tempArray, 1)
# 0.000004 seconds
```

I was not able to narrow down where or why it happens, but it feels like a bug.

```

Julia installed with juliaup.

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Compat.jl/issues/800","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Compat.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Compat.jl/issues/800/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Compat.jl/issues/800/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Compat.jl/issues/800/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/Compat.jl/issues/800","id":1806351247,"node_id":"I_kwDOAX1maM5rqruP","number":800,"title":"`@constfield` for `const` fields?","user":{"login":"Seelengrab","id":11753998,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjExNzUzOTk4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/11753998?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab","html_url":"https://github.com/Seelengrab","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2023-07-15T22:14:37Z","updated_at":"2024-08-07T08:08:39Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Hi!

I have this in one of my packages:

```julia
"""
    @constfield foo::Int

A macro providing compatibility for `const` fields in mutable structs.
Gives a `const` `Expr` if supported, otherwise is a noop and just returns the field.
"""
macro constfield(ex::Expr)
        (ex.head == Symbol("::") && length(ex.args) == 2) || throw(ArgumentError("`@constfield` only supports expressions of the form `field::Type`!"))
    ex = esc(ex)
    if VERSION < v"1.8"
        ex
    else
        Expr(:const, ex)
    end
end
```

and wanted to ask whether there's interest to have this macro in Compat? It's used like this:

```julia
struct Foo
     @constfield foo::Int
end
```

The idea is to have a noop (if unsupported) replacement for `const foo::Int` in struct definitions.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Compat.jl/issues/800/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Compat.jl/issues/800/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Compat.jl/issues/comments/2272876046","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/Compat.jl/issues/800#issuecomment-2272876046","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Compat.jl/issues/800","id":2272876046,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAX1maM6HeVYO","user":{"login":"Seelengrab","id":11753998,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjExNzUzOTk4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/11753998?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab","html_url":"https://github.com/Seelengrab","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-08-07T08:08:37Z","updated_at":"2024-08-07T08:08:37Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Yeah, stdlib"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-08-13T16:15:05Z","updated_at":"2024-08-14T07:22:40Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"It's a little strange and confusing that `@test f() kwarg=value` means two very different things:

* if `kwarg` is `skip` or `broken`, then those are flags to `@test` itself
* otherwise, the call is transformed into `@test f(; kwarg=value)`

This is mostly used for `@test x ≈ y atol=0.1` and the like, which is indeed handy.  But it'd also be nice to use keywords in arbitrary and extensible ways for custom test result types.

My real motivation here is that I'd like to be able to have a `Test.Result` subtype that could support other keywords beyond just `skip` or `broken`.  For example, many testing frameworks allow customizing the displayed name of a particular test — it'd be great if a custom `Test.Result` could choose to handle a `displayname` kwarg that would be ignored otherwise.  But with the current semantics, that's intractable.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55484/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55484/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2288031381","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55484#issuecomment-2288031381","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55484","id":2288031381,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6IYJaV","user":{"login":"Seelengrab","id":11753998,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjExNzUzOTk4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/11753998?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab","html_url":"https://github.com/Seelengrab","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Seelengrab/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-08-14T07:22:39Z","updated_at":"2024-08-14T07:22:39Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"> For example, many testing frameworks allow customizing the displayed name of a particular test — it'd be great if a custom Test.Result could choose to handle a displayname kwarg that would be ignored otherwise. As I mentioned in a review comment above, doing extremely short busy sleeps is appropriate in some situations. Not all of those situations benefit from doing "something else" either, especially if those solutions have either more overhead (`yield`) or aren't architecturally feasible. For example, in [StyledApple.jl](https://github.com/Seelengrab/StyledApple.jl/blob/15e3240468bfdc1169afc6ae71191d0599384102/apple.jl#L57-L60) I really do need a busy loop for alignment to 16ms blocks, since yielding is potentially too expensive if the currently processed frame takes 15.8ms, for example. If that's the expected behavior, i.e. `sleep` is guaranteed to yield to the scheduler, that should be documented. As is, the docstring doesn't specify one way or another, and other languages do the exact same thing as this PR would 🤷

> Why isn't it feasible? You can manually select tasks to run (yieldto or yield(t)) and switch to them without going through our scheduler, or pin tasks to threads.

I can't prevent tasks from yielding to the julia scheduler when they're yielding themselves, e.g. when they encounter a lock or perform I/O and need to wait on libuv, or simply call `sleep` themselves. I could provide custom implementations of all of that, but that still doesn't prevent unaware library code from yielding to the wrong scheduler when they're yielding themselves.

This is also one of the reasons why I haven't added testing of threaded statemachines to Supposition.jl, since the julia scheduler is not deterministic in a controllable way. See [this article](https://seelengrab.github.io/articles/Antithesis%20is%20not%20enough/) on my blog for why I'd love to have that.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2294619708/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

This PR though would only affect the cases where the scheduler overhead/libuv doesn't give better precision anyway, in which case it clearly is the right behavior. The task is blocked for a duration so short that it's not generally worth it to actually switch execution contexts.

> and I don't really think we should.

I have yet to see a good argument for why that is, other than the unspecific "would be better served by other approaches". I've already linked a project of mine where those other approaches aren't a good fit either and upthread people also agree that this is generally a (sometimes needed) good tool.

So I ask again: please consider usecases other than the one you're used to.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2295378463/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

iteration for `OrdinalRange{Int64}` and equivalent `Vector{Int64}` are different:
```
julia> collect(Iterators.rest(1:2, 1)) # OrdinalRange
1-element Vector{Int64}:
 2
```
while
```
 julia> collect(Iterators.rest(collect(1:2), 1)) # Vector
2-element Vector{Int64}:
 1
 2
```
Result probably should be exactly the same here.

Dear Julia,
My C++ application is using semaphore and it is called from Julia. However, it hangs after semaphore wait (sem_wait) is called when there is sleep() function. If I remove the sleep() function call from the code, it hangs during the wait(task1). 


1. Output of versioninfo()
```
julia> versioninfo()
Julia Version 1.10.5
Commit 6f3fdf7b362 (2024-08-27 14:19 UTC)
Build Info:
  Official https://julialang.org/ release
Platform Info:
  OS: Linux (x86_64-linux-gnu)
  CPU: 64 × AMD Ryzen Threadripper 3970X 32-Core Processor
  WORD_SIZE: 64
  LIBM: libopenlibm
  LLVM: libLLVM-15.0.7 (ORCJIT, znver2)
Threads: 1 default, 0 interactive, 1 GC (on 64 virtual cores)
Environment:
  JULIA_VERSION = 1.10.5
  LD_LIBRARY_PATH = /home/nqx/packages/julia-1.10.5/lib:/home/nqx/install/lib:/home/nqx/install/lib:

```
2. How you installed Julia:
I have installed by downloading the Linux x86 (64) binary
3. MWE
c_code.c:
```
#include <stdio.h>
#include <pthread.h>
#include <semaphore.h>

sem_t sem;
void c_sem_init() {
    sem_init(&sem, 0, 0);  // Initialize semaphore with a value of 0
}
void c_function_with_wait() {
    int res = sem_wait(&sem);
    if (res != 0) {
        perror("sem_wait failed");
    }
}
void c_function_with_post() {
    int res = sem_post(&sem);
    if (res != 0) {
        perror("sem_post failed");
    }
}
``` 

julia_c_code.jl:
```
# Define the C function signature
using Base.Threads;

function wait_on_semaphore()
    ccall((:c_function_with_wait, "libinfinite.so"), Cvoid, ())
end

function post_to_semaphore()
    ccall((:c_function_with_post, "libinfinite.so"), Cvoid, ())
end

gc_state = @ccall(jl_gc_safe_enter()::Int8)
ccall((:c_sem_init, "libinfinite.so"), Cvoid, ())
# Create tasks that call the respective functions
task1 = @spawn wait_on_semaphore()
println(Core.stdout, "Waiting on semaphore")
sleep(1)  # Ensure task1 runs and waits on the semaphore

println(Core.stdout, "Now posting on semaphore")
task2 = @spawn post_to_semaphore()
println(Core.stdout, "Posted on semaphore")

# Wait for both tasks to complete
wait(task1)
wait(task2)
@ccall(jl_gc_safe_leave(gc_state::Int8)::Cvoid)
```

Makefile:
```
build:
    gcc -g -O0 -fPIC -shared  -o libinfinite.so c_code.c -I$(JULIA)/include/julia -L$(JULIA)/lib -ljulia -lpthread
run:
    julia julia_c_code.jl
```

Output:
```
Waiting on semaphore
```

Please help to resolve this issue.

That being said, I believe the issue here is that you're running julia with one thread:

```
Threads: 1 default, 0 interactive, 1 GC (on 64 virtual cores)
```

Since the thread is blocked in the ccall, it will never yield back to the julia scheduler, which means that the julia scheduler can't schedule your main task (where you called `sleep` on) again.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2320220089/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/comments/1738159692","pull_request_review_id":2271510946,"id":1738159692,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOABkWpM5nmjZM","diff_hunk":"@@ -1116,3 +1121,74 @@ function get_max_methods_for_module(mod::Module)
     max_methods < 0 && return nothing
     return max_methods
 end
+
+"""
+    Future{T}
+
+Delayed return value for a value of type `T`, similar to RefValue{T}, but
+explicitly represents completed as a `Bool` rather than as `isdefined`.
+Set once with `f[] = v` and accessed with `f[]` afterwards.
+
+Can also be constructed with the `completed` flag value and a closure to
+produce `x`, as well as the additional arguments to avoid always capturing the
+same couple of values.
+"""
+mutable struct Future{T}
+    x::T
+    completed::Bool
+    Future{T}() where {T} = (f = new{T}(); f.completed = false; f)
+    Future{T}(x) where {T} = new{T}(x, true)
+    Future(x::T) where {T} = new{T}(x, true)
+end

compiler to be stackless","user":{"login":"vtjnash","id":330950,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMzMDk1MA==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/330950?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash","html_url":"https://github.com/vtjnash","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"This change ensures the compiler uses very little stack, making it compatible with running on any arbitrary system stack size and depths much more reliably. It also could be further modified now to easily add various forms of pause-able/resumable inference, since there is no implicit state on the stack--everything is local and explicit now.

Whereas before, less than 900 frames would crash in less than a second:
```
$ time ./julia -e 'f(::Val{N}) where {N} = N <= 0 ? 0 : f(Val(N - 1)); f(Val(1000))'
Warning: detected a stack overflow; program state may be corrupted, so further execution might be unreliable.
Internal error: during type inference of
f(Base.Val{1000})
Encountered stack overflow.
This might be caused by recursion over very long tuples or argument lists.

[23763] signal 6: Abort trap: 6
in expression starting at none:1
__pthread_kill at /usr/lib/system/libsystem_kernel.dylib (unknown line)
Allocations: 1 (Pool: 1; Big: 0); GC: 0
Abort trap: 6

real	0m0.233s
user	0m0.165s
sys	0m0.049s
````

Now: it is effectively unlimited, as long as you are willing to wait for it:
```
$ time ./julia -e 'f(::Val{N}) where {N} = N <= 0 ? 0 : f(Val(N - 1)); f(Val(50000))'
info: inference of f(Base.Val{50000}) from f(Base.Val{N}) where {N} exceeding 2500 frames (may be slow).
info: inference of f(Base.Val{50000}) from f(Base.Val{N}) where {N} exceeding 5000 frames (may be slow).
info: inference of f(Base.Val{50000}) from f(Base.Val{N}) where {N} exceeding 10000 frames (may be slow).
info: inference of f(Base.Val{50000}) from f(Base.Val{N}) where {N} exceeding 20000 frames (may be slow).
info: inference of f(Base.Val{50000}) from f(Base.Val{N}) where {N} exceeding 40000 frames (may be slow).
real	7m4.988s

$ time ./julia -e 'f(::Val{N}) where {N} = N <= 0 ? 0 : f(Val(N - 1)); f(Val(1000))'
real	0m0.214s
user	0m0.164s
sys	0m0.044s

$ time ./julia -e '@noinline f(::Val{N}) where {N} = N <= 0 ? GC.safepoint() : f(Val(N - 1)); f(Val(5000))'
info: inference of f(Base.Val{5000}) from f(Base.Val{N}) where {N} exceeding 2500 frames (may be slow).
info: inference of f(Base.Val{5000}) from f(Base.Val{N}) where {N} exceeding 5000 frames (may be slow).
real	0m8.609s
user	0m8.358s
sys	0m0.240s
```

compiler to be stackless","user":{"login":"vtjnash","id":330950,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMzMDk1MA==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/330950?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash","html_url":"https://github.com/vtjnash","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"This change ensures the compiler uses very little stack, making it compatible with running on any arbitrary system stack size and depths much more reliably. It also could be further modified now to easily add various forms of pause-able/resumable inference, since there is no implicit state on the stack--everything is local and explicit now.

Whereas before, less than 900 frames would crash in less than a second:
```
$ time ./julia -e 'f(::Val{N}) where {N} = N <= 0 ? 0 : f(Val(N - 1)); f(Val(1000))'
Warning: detected a stack overflow; program state may be corrupted, so further execution might be unreliable.
Internal error: during type inference of
f(Base.Val{1000})
Encountered stack overflow.
This might be caused by recursion over very long tuples or argument lists.

[23763] signal 6: Abort trap: 6
in expression starting at none:1
__pthread_kill at /usr/lib/system/libsystem_kernel.dylib (unknown line)
Allocations: 1 (Pool: 1; Big: 0); GC: 0
Abort trap: 6

real	0m0.233s
user	0m0.165s
sys	0m0.049s
````

Now: it is effectively unlimited, as long as you are willing to wait for it:
```
$ time ./julia -e 'f(::Val{N}) where {N} = N <= 0 ? 0 : f(Val(N - 1)); f(Val(50000))'
info: inference of f(Base.Val{50000}) from f(Base.Val{N}) where {N} exceeding 2500 frames (may be slow).
info: inference of f(Base.Val{50000}) from f(Base.Val{N}) where {N} exceeding 5000 frames (may be slow).
info: inference of f(Base.Val{50000}) from f(Base.Val{N}) where {N} exceeding 10000 frames (may be slow).
info: inference of f(Base.Val{50000}) from f(Base.Val{N}) where {N} exceeding 20000 frames (may be slow).
info: inference of f(Base.Val{50000}) from f(Base.Val{N}) where {N} exceeding 40000 frames (may be slow).
real	7m4.988s

$ time ./julia -e 'f(::Val{N}) where {N} = N <= 0 ? 0 : f(Val(N - 1)); f(Val(1000))'
real	0m0.214s
user	0m0.164s
sys	0m0.044s

$ time ./julia -e '@noinline f(::Val{N}) where {N} = N <= 0 ? GC.safepoint() : f(Val(N - 1)); f(Val(5000))'
info: inference of f(Base.Val{5000}) from f(Base.Val{N}) where {N} exceeding 2500 frames (may be slow).
info: inference of f(Base.Val{5000}) from f(Base.Val{N}) where {N} exceeding 5000 frames (may be slow).
real	0m8.609s
user	0m8.358s
sys	0m0.240s
```
